feedback.text: I think this is a great initiative, but it could be even better if it also covered consumer protections for digital purchases. Notably, "live service" video-games being sold as a regular product, only for the seller to later disable it permanently by shutting down their servers without offering any alternatives or refunds. The most egregious example being Ubisoft shutting down The Crew and denying refunds. These practices are especially worrying if you consider that allowing them to continue opens the door to similar scenarios with critical software like Operating Systems, or Enterprise Resource Planning software.
